The '''Mine Type C''', or '''Mn/T3''', was an uncrewed static defense weapon produced by the armaments manufacturer [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]].

==Description==
A stationary mine armed with an omni-directional [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannon]], the Mn/T3 was an inexpensive anti-intruder countermeasure. The laser cannon provided defense against [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], and the contact detonation system was effective against [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]], especially when the Mn/T3 was deployed in a large minefield.

The Mn/T3 was a far more formidable defensive weapon than either the [[Mine Type A|Mn/T1]] or the [[Mine Type B|Mn/T2]], because when its hull integrity was compromised by laser fire, it immediately launched a light countermeasure warhead at the attacking craft.

The Type 3 was also smaller and slimmer than the other types of Arakyd mines, making it even more difficult to target.

==Deployment==
The Type 3 was more expensive than the others, due to its more complex systems and warhead payload.

Thus, it was often deployed in smaller numbers in amongst a large spread of Type As or Type Bs. This was a strategic consideration, because, during a minesweeping mission a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] might not notice the Type Cs among the swarms of other mines. While a single one of these warheads was unlikely to destroy a shielded craft such as an [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]], if several of these mines were destroyed in quick succession by an unwary pilot, they might find themselves critically damaged by the volley of warheads released.

By the time the Empire developed the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defender]], Type C Mines were fitted with advanced concussion missiles, which proved extremely perilous at close range, even for fast and shielded craft. Only [[Missile Boat]]s could safely clear these minefields. From a far distance, they used one missile to destroy the mine itself, and a second missile to take out the newly launched warhead. As a last resort, Missile Boats could engage the SLAM acceleration system to outrun mine warheads that they could not intercept in time.<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref>

[[File:MineC2.jpg|thumb|left|A variant design of the Mn/T3.]]

==History==
Type 3 Mines were deployed by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] as part of the defensive screen for the [[MC40a light cruiser/Legends|MC40a light cruiser]] [[Lulsla (MC40a light cruiser)|''Lulsla'']]. It did hold up the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboat]] attack but not for long, as most Imperial pilots opted wisely to ignore the minefield itself, being able to launch [[Proton rocket/Legends|heavy rockets]] at the Rebel cruiser from a safe distance. [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]], however, had to brave the minefield in order to capture the Rebel officers attempting to escape their doomed vessel.<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter" />

[[Ali Tarrak]]'s TIE Defender factory was well defended by a minefield of Type C's, alongside her fighters, and the facilities own turbolasers and anti-warhead launcher. [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]] in his Missile Boat was instrumental in eliminating all of these defenses so unshielded [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] could safely make their bombing runs against the facility itself.<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter" />
